twitter_thread: 85% of expert consumers drop AI creative tools within 2 weeks, citing poor ROI.
CapCut users see 30% more content, but only with 5+ hours of dedicated training.
Small businesses average $1,200/month on AI tools, yet 60% don't see a return.
Don't assign AI tools to interns: 90% of successful implementations have a dedicated pro.
CapCut's 24/7 support is a major draw, with 80% of users citing it as a key factor.
CapCut's UI is intuitive, but 40% of users still need external tutorials to get started.
What's the most time you've invested in an AI tool before seeing real results? #aicreatives #smallbusiness
pack.linkedin: A recent survey found that 85% of expert consumers reject AI creative tools within 2 weeks, largely due to poor ROI. In contrast, small businesses that successfully implement tools like CapCut see a 30% increase in content production, but this requires at least 5 hours of dedicated training. The average monthly spend on AI tools is $1,200, yet 60% of businesses don't see a return. Assigning a dedicated professional to the tool is crucial, as 90% of successful implementations have one. What has been your experience with AI creative tools, and what factors have contributed to their success or failure in your organization?
